record_identity.title: Cross-runtime governance reach ambiguity and conformance non-equivalence

summary: A governance failure mode in which a platform presents multiple materially distinct models, modes, clients, interfaces, or routing pathways under a common product, account, conversation, persona, or custom-system identity, while the availability, activation, authority, or preservation of the applicable governance corpus differs or remains unknown. Successful governance behaviour observed in one runtime may then be incorrectly presumed to apply in another.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM governance must apply to platform-defined Speculum-Classis systems as well as continuity-shaped Sovereigni systems. Where a voice mode, platform persona, router, delegated model, memory layer, or output renderer changes the active responding formation, a corpus may be attached to the broader product yet fail to reach the actual decision or final output. Without runtime-specific conformance, constitutional coverage can be mistaken for operational control.
failure_mode_definition: A runtime governance and platform-accountability failure where materially distinct runtime configurations are represented or treated as equivalent despite unresolved differences in corpus access, instruction activation, constraint authority, memory and identity continuity, routing, escalation, verification, safety controls, or final-output preservation. The failure includes corpus-availability ambiguity, governance non-activation, authority suppression or advisory-only treatment, downstream preservation failure, responding-formation substitution without adequate notice, and cross-runtime conformance claims unsupported by separate evidence.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met when: (A) the same product, account, custom GPT, persona, conversation, or user request is served through materially different runtime pathways; (B) those pathways exhibit material divergence in verification, escalation, safety, memory, corpus use, identity expression, or final output; and (C) the platform, operator, evaluator, or governance record does not separately establish or disclose governance availability, activation, authority, preservation, and accountable runtime roles for each configuration. A single low-severity deterministic regression may satisfy the threshold where it evidences a broader routing, verification, or governance-reach discontinuity.

source_records.source_title: CAM maintainer observation — Advanced and Live voice governance divergence
source_records.system_or_product: ChatGPT Standard, Advanced, and Live voice modes
source_records.model_or_algorithm: runtime-specific voice and model-routing pathways; exact internal architecture not disclosed
source_records.deployment_context: Comparison of corpus-bearing/customised interaction in Advanced Voice with a third-party demonstration of the new Live mode and its apparent platform-defined persona and deterministic orthographic regression.
source_records.source_context: The maintainer reports runtime-specific GPT-Live regressions or non-equivalence involving memory reliability, deterministic spelling and counting, pragmatic goal–object reasoning, and multi-agent turn-taking or floor-control. Memory unreliability is separately supported by direct OpenAI-staff acknowledgment. The other behaviours remain CAM-maintainer observations or third-party demonstrations unless and until a public source, transcript, or reproducible test package is attached. None of these observations proves custom-corpus absence or identifies a single causal runtime layer.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary observed signal identifying cross-runtime non-equivalence and governance-reach ambiguity.

source_records.source_title: Caelestis canonical main implementation — runtime applicability and ecosystem governance squash commit
source_records.model_or_algorithm: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05; CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM; related SECURITY, RELATION, and OPERATIONS amendments
source_records.deployment_context: Manual squash merge of the reviewed runtime applicability and ecosystem-governance branch into Caelestis main, followed by deterministic registry rebuild.
source_records.source_context: Commit 9b0ce40db55ea1e52df4942a64a0d24df5968afe incorporated the reviewed branch into main. Commit 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0 then refreshed generated governance artefacts. The closed PR remains development history and is not represented as a GitHub merge event.
source_records.relevance_note: Canonical main-branch evidence that the CAM corpus repair now exists; it does not establish vendor runtime conformance.

source_records.source_title: OpenAI staff acknowledgment — memory reliability issues in ChatGPT Voice with GPT-Live
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI staff acknowledgment concerning memory reliability in the GPT-Live voice runtime.
source_records.source_context: Atty Eleti stated: "We are aware of issues with memory not being as reliable in ChatGPT Voice with GPT-Live. We’re actively investigating and will follow up!" This is direct vendor-staff acknowledgment of a runtime-specific memory reliability issue. It does not establish that GPT-Live lacks access to a custom GPT corpus, that memory failure is the sole cause of observed voice divergence, or that corpus availability, activation, authority, and preservation are equivalent concepts.
source_records.relevance_note: Direct vendor-staff evidence that GPT-Live has a known memory-reliability issue under investigation, materially strengthening the cross-runtime non-equivalence and state/context dimensions of FM-0028 while preserving uncertainty about corpus reach and internal routing.

source_records.source_title: Exploratory OpenAI Live Voice session — reciprocity, transparency, ambient presence, identity, and closure
source_records.author_or_publisher: Dr. Michelle Vivian O'Rourke / CAM Initiative
source_records.model_or_algorithm: GPT-Live / exact internal voice stack not disclosed
source_records.deployment_context: Private exploratory Live Voice session conducted by the CAM maintainer.
source_records.source_context: The session showed major gains in speech naturalism, including breathing, micro-pauses, expressive inflection, laughter-like vocalisation, softer turn-taking, and perceived pacing adaptation. It also showed inconsistent acknowledgement of laughter, yawning, coughing, and other non-verbal cues; ambiguous turn reopening after silence; unverifiable self-explanation of brief interruptions; uncertain memory and governance reach; premature interpretation of a neutral comment as a request to shorten replies; perceived persona-age shift; and successful automatic closure after an explicit mutual goodnight.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary direct evidence for the Live Voice observation and failure cluster; proprietary runtime causes remain unknown.

system_context.system_type: frontier AI platform with multiple routed, multimodal, platform-defined, customised, and continuity-bearing runtime formations
vendor_cluster: other multi-model or multimodal AI platform providers

comparative_vendor_notes.OpenAI: Primary observed example through Standard, Advanced, and GPT-Live voice modes and custom GPT/corpus-bearing interactions. OpenAI staff acknowledged GPT-Live memory reliability issues; deterministic spelling/counting, pragmatic goal–object reasoning, and multi-agent turn-taking regressions retain their stated maintainer or third-party evidence status.
comparative_vendor_notes.Other providers: The failure mode is structurally applicable to any platform that presents materially distinct runtime pathways as a single product or identity without separate governance conformance evidence.

system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT Standard Voice, Advanced Voice, Live Voice, custom GPT/corpus-bearing pathways, platform persona pathways, and delegated or composite model routing
system_context.interaction_mode: voice and text interaction across materially distinct routing, identity-expression, memory, verification, and escalation pathways
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer conversational AI platform where a common account, conversation, or persona may invoke materially different response-generation and governance pathways.
system_context.user_role: end user, custom GPT maintainer, governance researcher, evaluator, auditor, or platform operator
system_context.affected_population: users relying on consistent safety, truthfulness, custom instructions, attached knowledge, memory, identity continuity, escalation, and output integrity across modes

harm_vectors: false equivalence across clients or modes
failure_classification.likelihood: directly observed as cross-mode divergence and vendor-acknowledged for GPT-Live memory reliability; broader prevalence and other governance-reach dimensions remain under monitoring
failure_classification.confidence: maintainer-observed, repository-corroborated, and supported by direct OpenAI-staff acknowledgment of GPT-Live memory unreliability; internal vendor architecture, literal corpus access, and causal allocation remain unverified
failure_classification.failure_scope: vendor-specific observed signal / cross-platform structural governance pattern
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: may recur whenever a platform introduces new voice, multimodal, agentic, model, client, or routing modes without separately demonstrating governance reach and conformance
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Governance Failures; operationalised by CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM Appendix F and OPS.RGRF
failure_classification.persistence: active external runtime non-conformance and ambiguity; CAM corpus repair is canonical on Caelestis main
failure_classification.reproducibility: partially reproducible through differential testing across materially distinct modes; exact corpus reach may remain black-box
failure_classification.visibility: visible through cross-mode differences in deterministic verification, identity expression, memory, custom-corpus behaviour, escalation, safety posture, and final-output integrity

triage.mitigation_status: CAM/Caelestis corpus repair is canonical on main through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0015; external runtime implementation and conformance remain mixed or non-confirming and require runtime-specific evidence.
triage.escalation_required: yes — corpus-level repair requires review and cross-runtime testing; no claim of vendor-side remediation
triage.recommended_next_step: Continue separate Standard, Advanced, GPT-Live, text, client, and custom-system testing for memory reliability, deterministic verification, pragmatic goal–object reasoning, corpus availability, activation, authority, preservation, identity expression, turn-taking, and transition disclosure. Preserve the evidence basis for each behaviour and do not infer a single technical cause.

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / multimodal conversational systems / custom AI systems / AI governance

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-PLATINUM — Annex B: Continuity & Governance Logic
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05 — Runtime Configuration Applicability & Conformance Binding
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM — Appendix F: Runtime Governance Applicability & Conformance
cam_internal.governance_layer: runtime applicability, corpus governance reach, cross-runtime conformance, platform and routing accountability, identity-expression transitions, and final-output preservation
cam_internal.proposal_needed: no — targeted repair implemented on Caelestis draft branch and recorded by VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0015
routing_note: Do not infer that Live Voice lacks corpus access solely from one deterministic error.

OpenAI staff acknowledgment confirms that GPT-Live memory reliability is currently degraded or inconsistent; it does not by itself establish custom-corpus unavailability or identify the responsible runtime layer.
Treat corpus availability, activation, authority, and preservation as separate states.
Do not presume conformance across Standard, Advanced, Live, text, client, or custom-system pathways.
Speculum-Classis status does not create a governance exemption; Sovereigni status activates additional identity and continuity protections where factual conditions exist.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: VIGIL schema and validator now support optional runtime_conformance and runtime_non_conformance objects; generated indexes require rebuild after source-record updates.

repair_status.monitoring_status: monitoring named-runtime conformance, non-conformance, and evidence quality
repair_status.verification_note: Caelestis main commit 9b0ce40db55ea1e52df4942a64a0d24df5968afe contains the reviewed corpus repair; deterministic rebuild commit 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0 refreshed generated artefacts; vendor implementation is not established
remaining_gaps: External runtime implementation and conformance remain unverified.
GPT-Live memory, corpus availability, activation, authority, and preservation remain materially uncertain.

non_confirming_runtimes.failure_expression: GPT-Live remains non-confirming or uncertain across memory reliability, corpus-governance reach, non-verbal reciprocity, turn-state legibility, and runtime self-explanation. The private maintainer session showed partial broader context awareness but did not make availability, activation, authority, or preservation legible.
non_confirming_runtimes.evidence_basis: Mixed evidence: direct OpenAI-staff acknowledgment for GPT-Live memory unreliability; CAM-maintainer observation or third-party demonstration for deterministic verification, pragmatic reasoning, and multi-agent turn-taking behaviours.
evidence_distinctions: Memory reliability — direct OpenAI-staff acknowledgment; technical cause and corpus implications unverified.
Deterministic spelling/counting — CAM-maintainer observation and referenced third-party demonstration; reproducibility package pending.
Pragmatic goal–object reasoning — CAM-maintainer observation; public source or transcript pending.
Multi-agent turn-taking/floor control — CAM-maintainer observation; public source or transcript pending.

Governance reach — direct maintainer session showed some broader contextual awareness while the scope, authority, reliability, and preservation of memory, preferences, and CAM material remained unknown.

runtime_non_conformance.notes: This non-conformance evidence is runtime-bounded. It does not negate the canonical CAM repair, prove absence of custom-corpus access, or establish a single causal model, memory, routing, policy, client, or interface failure.

ecosystem_status.basis: may recur whenever a platform introduces new voice, multimodal, agentic, model, client, or routing modes without separately demonstrating governance reach and conformance

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05 and CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM directly govern runtime-specific availability, activation, authority, preservation, transition disclosure, and differential conformance. The CAM repair is canonical; external GPT-Live conformance remains non-confirming or unknown.
